content.problemStatement:
Chef has a binary string SSS of length NNN. Chef also has QQQ queries.

Initially Chef has an empty basket. Chef processes all the queries successively.

After the iii-th query:


Chef adds the substring of SSS consisting of all the positions from LiL_iLi​ to RiR_iRi​ into the basket.
He asks you whether all the binary strings in the basket can be rearranged to form a binary number **divisible** by 333.
Note: A substring of a string is a contiguous subsequence of that string.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the length of the binary string SSS and the number of queries, respectively.
The next line contains a single binary string  — SSS.
The next QQQ lines describe the queries. The iii-th of these QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​, denoting substring of SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line after each query:



Print `YES`if all the binary strings in the basket can be rearranged to form a binary number **divisible** by 333 , otherwise print `NO`.

You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.output:
NO
YES
YES
YES
NO
YES
YES
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case:

After the first query the basket contains "101101101".
It cannot be rearranged to form any binary number divisible by 3.
After the second query the basket contains "101101101" and "101101101". It can be rearranged to form the binary number 101101101101101101 = 454545 (in decimal) divisible by 3.
After the third query the basket contains "101101101", "101101101" and "101011010110101". It can be rearranged to form the binary number 101011011011010110110110101101101 = 138913891389 (in decimal) divisible by 3.

For the second test case:

After the first query the basket contains "000000". It can be rearranged to form the binary number 000000 = 000 (in decimal) divisible by 3.
After the second query the basket contains "000000" and "010101". It cannot be rearranged to form any binary number divisible by 3.
After the third query the basket contains "000000", "010101" and "101010". It can be rearranged to form the binary number 100001100001100001 = 333333 (in decimal) divisible by 3.
